types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: BigDecimal

inputFields.name: searchInPastCompanies
inputFields.description: If set to true, match company against both current and past positions. Defaults to false.

inputFields.name: strict
inputFields.description: If set to true, all input company details must match some existing companies. If the company doesn't exists, maybe due to bad data, the search result will be empty. Defaults to false.

fields.description: Company Id

fields.name: jobChanges
fields.description: Returns a list of jobchanges. Note that this collection will be empty if the task is not yet completed.

fields.name: icpDefinitionSnapshot
fields.description: A snapshot of the ICP/Persona as they were defined at the time of this run.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The present status of the task.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: The timestamp of when this task was created.

fields.name: monthsBack
fields.description: The number of months in the past to look for job changes. This value will be rounded to the 1st of the month. For the exact start date, see `jobStartDateFrom`

fields.name: jobStartDateFrom
fields.description: The exact start date to look for job changes.

fields.name: maxContactsPerCompany
fields.description: The maximum number of contacts that will be returned per company

fields.name: icps
fields.description: The ICP/Persona filter

fields.name: filters
fields.description: Filters to narrow down job detections scope.

fields.name: error
fields.description: The error message if the task is in a failed state.

fields.name: errorCode
fields.description: The error code if the task is in a failed state.

fields.name: isCancelled
fields.description: True if the task was manually cancelled before completion.

fields.name: timeoutAt
fields.description: Time when the job will be forcefully complete if not finished

fields.name: isTimeout
fields.description: True if this was timeout

fields.name: suppressedContacts
fields.description: Number of job detections that were dropped due to privacy suppression settings

fields.name: warning
fields.description: The warning message if the task finished with a partial success

enumValues.name: Company
enumValues.description: Represents all datapoints of a company.

enumValues.name: Person
enumValues.description: Represents a unified `Person` datapoint. Will only be present if `PersonEmail` and `PersonPhone` are not.

enumValues.name: PersonEmail
enumValues.description: Represents an email datapoint of a person. Will only be present if `Person` is not.

enumValues.name: PersonPhone
enumValues.description: Represents a phone datapoint of a person. Will only be present if `Person` is not.

fields.name: name
fields.description: A human readable name for the plan. e.g. Enterprise Annual

fields.name: product
fields.description: The product that this plan is for

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the plan

fields.name: available
fields.description: The next billing period for this plan, null if the plan is inactive or will not reactivate

inputFields.name: workEmail
inputFields.description:
Work email. Must be a valid email address (RFC 5322); malformed values are
rejected with 400.

inputFields.name: emailStatus
inputFields.description:
Verification status of `workEmail`. Set this when the email has already been
vetted upstream (e.g., the prospect came from tool_SearchPeople with a
verified email). Allowed values: `Verified`, `VerifiedLikely`, `Unverified`.
Omit if unknown — do not default to `Unverified`. Other values (including
`Invalid`) are rejected with 400 on create; `Invalid` may still appear on
reads when set by the verify-email endpoints.

inputFields.name: phone
inputFields.description:
Mobile phone number. Plain string; format isn't validated. When `personId`
is also supplied, prospector-api will attempt to verify this number against
dataiq's records and emit `status: Verified` on the resulting prospect's
`mobilePhones` if the confidence score crosses the threshold.

inputFields.name: personId
inputFields.description:
dataiq stable person identifier. Optional. Required only to opt into the
phone-verification flow described under `phone` — when both `phone` and
`personId` are supplied, prospector-api gates a tyrion RUM check + dataiq
score lookup. Without `personId`, the phone is still saved but no badge is
emitted on read.

inputFields.name: seniority
inputFields.description:
Job seniority bucket. Allowed values: `VP`, `Manager`, `Director`,
`Executive`, `SeniorIndividualContributor`, `Other`. Other values are
rejected with 400 — when the source title doesn't map cleanly, use `Other`
rather than guessing.

inputFields.name: linkedinUrl
inputFields.description:
Prospect's LinkedIn profile URL. Must be a valid URL; malformed values are
rejected with 400.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description:
Prospect's address. All fields are optional; provide whatever you have.
`formatted` is the human-readable single-line form; the structured fields
(`streetLine1`/`streetLine2`/`city`/`state`/`zip`/`country`) populate the
parsed columns independently.

inputFields.name: notes
inputFields.description:
Free-form notes attached to the prospect. Max 5000 characters; longer values
are rejected with 400.

types.name: Json

types.name: LocalDate
types.description: A date without a time-zone in the ISO-8601 calendar system using the format 1970-01-01

types.name: Long
types.description: The `Long` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Long can represent values between -(2^63) and 2^63 - 1.

types.name: MobilePhoneStatus
types.description:
Wire-level phone verification status. Single positive value (`Verified`) is
emitted when prospector-api's score-based check confirms the number; absent
otherwise. Future positive values may be added; consumers should treat any
unrecognized value as "absent / unknown" rather than failing.

types.name: PlanStatus
types.description: An enum representing the status of a plan.

types.name: ProductName
types.description: A high level product name. This is used to delineate between subscriptions to different products.

fields.name: emailStatus
fields.description:
Verification status of `workEmail`. One of: `Verified`, `VerifiedLikely`,
`Unverified`, or `Invalid` (the latter is set by the verify-email endpoints
when an address fails verification — it can be present on saved prospects
whose email was checked after creation). May be null when the prospect was
saved without an email or without a verification verdict.

fields.name: mobilePhones
fields.description:
Mobile phone numbers attached to the prospect. Each entry carries the
number plus an optional `status` flag — `Verified` when prospector-api
was able to confirm the number against dataiq's records (score-driven,
threshold-gated), absent otherwise. Internal `LikelyAccurate` /
`VerifiedByUser` / `Edited` storage values collapse to a single wire
enum (`Verified`) or are omitted; do not surface other values.

fields.name: searchPeoplePreview
fields.description:
Lightweight preview to check whether a work email and/or phone can likely be produced for a person.
Does not charge credits. Returns the matched personId and the requested boolean flags.
At least one of `hasEmail` or `hasPhone` must be selected.

Input for `searchPeoplePreview`. Provide at least one of:

  - `email`

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.
